description: Opt-in PreToolUse CAUTION guard for sensitive production / config files. Copy the `hooks` block into .claude/settings.json to activate. It matches `Edit|Write` and inspects only the target file PATH (never the contents) read from the tool-call JSON on stdin. When the path looks like infrastructure or production config — `.env`, `.env.*`, `**/production*`, `*.tf`, `**/terraform/**`, `**/k8s/**`, `**/helm/**`, `Dockerfile.prod`, or a CI deploy workflow (`.github/workflows/*deploy*`) — it exits 2 to PAUSE the change and print a caution. This is a deliberate speed-bump, not a hard ban: the message asks you to confirm consciously and proceed yourself if the edit is intended (re-issue with intent, or temporarily disable the hook). It NEVER echoes file contents or secrets, NEVER auto-edits, deletes, or installs anything, and NEVER touches production directly — it only decides whether to warn on the pending tool call.

hooks.command: in=$(cat); path=$(printf '%s' "$in" | grep -oE '"file_path"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*"[^"]+"' | head -1 | sed -E 's/.*:[[:space:]]*"([^"]+)"/\1/'); base=$(basename "$path"); printf '%s' "$path" | grep -Eq '(^|/)\.env($|\.)|(^|/)production[^/]*|\.tf$|(^|/)terraform/|(^|/)k8s/|(^|/)helm/|(^|/)Dockerfile\.prod$|(^|/)\.github/workflows/[^/]*deploy[^/]*' || exit 0; echo "[production-file-guard] CAUTION: '$base' looks like a sensitive production or infrastructure config file (env / Terraform / Kubernetes / Helm / prod Dockerfile / CI deploy workflow). Changes here can affect real environments, credentials, or deploys. This is a caution, not a hard block — review the change consciously, ensure you have a rollback, and re-run it yourself if it is intended (CLAUDE.md §8). Secrets must stay in a secret manager, never in committed files." >&2; exit 2
